top_signals.title: Energean Slashes Dividend After Israel Gas Shutdown Cuts Output
top_signals.summary: UK-based oil and gas producer Energean plc on Wednesday slashed its first-quarter dividend and full-year production outlook following a 41-day halt to production at a gas field offshore Israel due to the Middle East conflict. Energean suspended production at the Karish platform offshore Israel in early March under orders by the Israeli Energy Ministry. Operations resumed on April 9, after the ministry instructed the company to restore production following the announcement of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire. The Israeli government’s go-ahead permitted…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Target beats Wall Street estimates, hikes sales outlook as shoppers start to return
top_signals.summary: Target has been struggling with a sales slump and decreased customer traffic, though CEO Michael Fiddelke has said the retailer is poised for a turnaround.

top_signals.title: Former Tory MP set to call Sunak in libel spat with Hancock
top_signals.summary: The Court of Appeal is set to hear the latest chapter in a libel claim brought by expelled Tory MP Andrew Bridgen against former ex-health minister Matt Hancock MP.  The legal battle between the ex-politicians erupted in January 2023, when Hancock posted on social media about Bridgen after the Tories suspended him over a “disgraceful” [...]
